channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Context Compression Is Not One Thing: Readable Symbolic Re-expression vs. Coherent Summary at Matched Budget
item.description: arXiv:2606.14875v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study context compression for multi-hop question answering with small language models. We propose Telegraph English, a readable symbolic format that rewrites retrieved passages into structured entity-relation statements, preserving reasoning eviden...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> compression, summarization, context, reasoning, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Simplifying the Modeling of Arbitrary Conditionals in Natural Language
item.description: arXiv:2606.14943v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Causal Transformers model sequences through an autoregressive factorization of the joint distribution, which enables efficient left-to-right decoding and conditional likelihood computation. However, they cannot tractably sample from or evaluate arbitr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, LLM, context, GPT, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ReportQA: QA-Based Radiology Report Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2606.15037v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Radiology report evaluation is essential for advancing automated report generation. Natural language generation metrics have limited clinical relevance. Clinical efficacy (CE) metrics evaluate important medical findings, but focus mainly on presence a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> image, vision, template, large language model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Equity with Efficiency: An Empirical Study of Tokenizers for Multilingual Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.15044v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multilingual large language models (LLMs) depend on subword tokenization to bridge discrete text and continuous neural representation. State-of-the-art multilingual LLMs often use Byte-level Byte-Pair Encoding (BPE) tokenizers that structurally favor ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> compression, large language model, transformer, LLM, reasoning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Definition of Good Explanations and the Challenges Explaining LLM Outputs
item.description: arXiv:2606.14838v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: How to define a good explanation is a long-standing philosophical debate which has found recent renewed interest in the context of AI outputs. Explainability is crucial for AI adoption in many contexts, but in order to produce good explanations of AI ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, paper, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Dr-DCI: Scaling Direct Corpus Interaction via Dynamic Workspace Expansion
item.description: arXiv:2606.14885v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agentic search over large corpora relies on retriever-mediated interfaces (e.g., BM25 or ColBERT) for scalable candidate discovery. While effective at ranking relevant documents, these interfaces expose evidence only as ranked results or bounded docum...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> tool, analysis, RAG, context, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AI Engram: In Search of Memory Traces in Artificial Intelligence
item.description: arXiv:2606.14997v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Memory formation is fundamental to intelligence, yet whether deep neural networks preserve identifiable memory traces analogous to biological memory units remains an open question. This work introduces a geometric framework to identify such "AI engram...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, RAG, LLM, framework, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Metric Match: A Subset Selection Approach to Evaluating LLM Judge Reliability
item.description: arXiv:2606.15029v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM judges are used to reduce the need for costly human labor in evaluating open-ended text generation. However, the reliability of these judges depends critically on their alignment with human raters -- a property that itself depends on costly human ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, LLM, ICL, alignment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: OSGuard: A Benchmark for Safety in Computer-Use Agents
item.description: arXiv:2606.15034v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Computer-use agents are increasingly evaluated by whether they complete realistic desktop and web tasks. However, task success alone can miss failures in which an agent reaches the nominal goal through an unsafe shortcut. We introduce OSGuard, a dual-...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> multimodal, context, augmented, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GRASP: Gradient-Aligned Sequential Parameter Transfer for Memory-Efficient Multi-Source Learning
item.description: arXiv:2606.14900v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-source transfer learning faces a fundamental scalability bottleneck: existing approaches require either loading all K source models into memory simultaneously during parameter fusion, requiring O(K) memory, or deploying all models at inference t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, product, fine-tuning, experiment, alignment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Remember, Don't Re-read: Stateful ReAct Agents for Token-Efficient Autonomous Experimentation
item.description: arXiv:2606.14945v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The autoresearch pattern enables autonomous experimentation by having a large language model (LLM) iteratively modify code to optimize a target metric. Its stateless design, however, reconstructs experimental context from scratch at every iteration, i...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> large language model, paper, tool, LLM, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Comparative Study of Graph Neural Network Layer Selection for Interaction Modelling in Driving Trajectory Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2606.14956v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Autonomous driving systems rely on precise trajectory prediction to plan safe and efficient movement. Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have become a promising approach for modelling spatiotemporal interactions among road agents. However, designing GNN arc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, attention, arxiv, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Leveraging Physiological Signals to Predict Exam Outcomes with Machine Learning
item.description: arXiv:2606.14960v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This study investigates the application of machine learning models to predict exam outcomes using physiological data collected during examination sessions. Physiological stress indicators, including electrodermal activity, heart rate, and skin tempera...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, vector, RAG, transformer, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Evaluating the Robustness of Proof Autoformalization in Lean 4
item.description: arXiv:2606.14867v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Proof autoformalization aims to translate a mathematical informal proof written in natural language into a formal proof in a formal language such as Lean~4. Several works have developed LLM-based models for proof autoformalization. However, existing e...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Are Online Skill and Memory Modules Always Worth Their Tokens? A Budget-Constrained Study of Web Agents
item.description: arXiv:2606.15017v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Online web agents often augment a base actor with memory, workflow, or skill modules. These modules can improve performance, but they also consume test-time tokens, a cost rarely reported alongside the actor's inference cost. We study online augmentat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, reasoning, GPT, arxiv, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Trust Between AI Agents: Measuring Formation, Breakage, and Recovery, with Implications for Governing Multi-Agent Systems
item.description: arXiv:2606.14923v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As language-model agents increasingly work in teams, each agent must decide how much to trust its teammates. Yet we lack a standard way to measure trust between AI agents. We propose a behavioral measure based on costly verification. In a cooperative ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> memory, GPT, framework, arxiv, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Benchmarking Instance-Dependent Label Noise with Controlled Corruptions
item.description: arXiv:2606.14965v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Synthetic instance-dependent label noise (IDN) benchmarks are widely used to evaluate noisy-label learning methods, yet existing approaches typically generate noise through imperfect annotators or classifier raters, leaving the source of ambiguity imp...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, arxiv, study, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PhoneHarness: Harnessing Phone-Use Agents through Mixed GUI, CLI, and Tool Actions
item.description: arXiv:2606.14832v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Phone agents are increasingly expected to complete real mobile workflows rather than merely predict the next screen action. However, much of the current mobile-agent literature still evaluates agents primarily as GUI controllers that observe a screen,...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, study, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Relational Structural Causal Models
item.description: arXiv:2606.14892v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: An artificial intelligence must have a model of its environment that is causal, supporting reasoning about interventions and counterfactuals, and also combinatorial, supporting generalization to unseen combinations of objects. In this work, we formall...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, arxiv, study, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: GRAPE: Guided Parameter-Space Evolution for Compact Adversarial Robustness
item.description: arXiv:2606.14865v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Adversarial Training (AT) improves neural network robustness, but most methods train a fixed parameter space from the start. This paper asks whether the order in which parameters become optimizable can affect the final robust solution, even when the f...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, release, framework, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
